items.title: Latest Xloader Obfuscation Methods and Network Protocol
items.description: Xloader is an information stealing malware family that evolved from Formbook and targets web browsers, email clients, and File Transfer Protocol (FTP) applications. Additionally, Xloader may execute arbitrary commands and download second-stage payloads on an infected system. The author of Xloader continues to update the codebase, with the most recent observed version being 8.7. Since version 8.1,
items.ai_summary: Xloader is an information stealing malware that targets web browsers, email clients, and FTP applications, with capabilities to execute arbitrary commands and download second-stage payloads. The malware uses obfuscation methods to evade detection, making it challenging for defenders to identify and mitigate. To defend against Xloader, it is essential to implement robust endpoint detection and response systems to detect and block suspicious activities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1027| B[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    B -->|T1608| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1027| E[Persistence]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: North Korea-Nexus Threat Actor Compromises Widely Used Axios NPM Package in Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) is tracking an active software supply chain attack targeting the popular Node Package Manager (NPM) package "axios." Between March 31, 2026, 00:21 and 03:20 UTC, an attacker introduced a malicious dependency named "plain-crypto-js" into axios NPM releases versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. Axios is the most popular JavaScript library used to simplify HTTP requests,
items.ai_summary: The North Korea-Nexus threat actor compromised the widely used Axios NPM package in a supply chain attack by introducing a malicious dependency named 'plain-crypto-js' into axios NPM releases versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. This attack can lead to unauthorized access and data breaches. To defend against this threat, developers should immediately update or remove the affected axios versions and monitor their dependencies for suspicious activity.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Stranger Strings: Yurei Ransomware Operator Toolkit Exposed
items.description: Active since September 2025, Yurei is a double extortion ransomware campaign. The operators run their own Tor data leak site with a low number of victims listed at the time of writing. It is reportedly derived from Prince Ransomware, an open-source ransomware family written in Go. Check Point researchers noted that all samples were first submitted to VirusTotal from Morocco, and that one sample di
items.ai_summary: Yurei ransomware, a double extortion campaign, uses T1190 to exploit public-facing applications, T1041 to exfiltrate data, and T1486 to encrypt data for impact. This toolkit is reportedly derived from Prince Ransomware. Defenders should monitor for T1190 and T1041 to prevent initial access and data exfiltration.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1041| C[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Cisco Talos: Qilin EDR killer infection chain
items.description: Endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools are widely deployed and far more capable than traditional antivirus. As a result, attackers use EDR killers to disable or bypass them. The malicious “msimg32.dll” used in Qilin ransomware attacks, which is a multi-stage infection chain targeting EDR systems. It can terminate over 300 different EDR drivers from almost every vendor in the market.
items.ai_summary: Qilin ransomware uses a multi-stage infection chain to target EDR systems, disabling or bypassing them to maintain persistence. The infection chain terminates over 300 different EDR drivers from various vendors. Defenders should prioritize updating EDR tools and monitoring for suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1104| B[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Blurred Lines: AdTech Abuse Delivers Browser Hijackers Through the Microsoft Store
items.description: A newly uncovered campaign abuses the Trillion (formerly Trellian) AdTech network, mimicking the flow of a Traffic Direction System (TDS) to trick visitors of typo-squatted domains into downloading Microsoft Store apps that contain browser hijacking malware. While the abuse of AdTech networks to deliver malware isn’t new, this campaign highlights incredibly similar tactics to VexTrio and previous
items.ai_summary: A threat actor is abusing the Trillion AdTech network to deliver browser hijacking malware through Microsoft Store apps, mimicking a Traffic Direction System to trick users into downloading malicious apps. This campaign shares similarities with VexTrio and previous incidents. Defenders should monitor AdTech network activity and scrutinize Microsoft Store app downloads for suspicious behavior.

graph LR

items.title: Signed malware impersonating workplace apps deploys RMM backdoors
items.description: Multiple phishing campaigns were identified using workplace meeting lures, PDF attachments, and abuse of legitimate binaries to deliver signed malware. The attacks used digitally signed executables masquerading as legitimate software to install remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools like ScreenConnect, Tactical RMM, and Mesh Agent. These tools enabled attackers to establish persistence and m
items.ai_summary: The threat involves signed malware masquerading as legitimate workplace software, delivered through phishing campaigns, to deploy RMM backdoors. This allows attackers to establish persistence and gain remote access. The most important defender action is to implement robust email filtering and user education to prevent initial phishing attempts.

items.title: Securing the Supply Chain: How SentinelOne's AI EDR Stops the ...
items.description: On March 31, 2026, a North Korean state actor hijacked the npm credentials of the primary Axios maintainer and published two backdoored releases that deployed a cross-platform remote access trojan (RAT) to Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. Axios is the most widely used HTTP client in the JavaScript ecosystem, with approximately 100 million weekly downloads and a presence in roughly 80% of cloud a
items.ai_summary: A North Korean state actor hijacked Axios npm credentials, publishing backdoored releases that deployed a cross-platform RAT to Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. This supply chain compromise leveraged T1195 and T1219 tactics, impacting approximately 100 million weekly downloads. Defenders should prioritize validating software dependencies and monitoring for suspicious network activity.

items.title: A Technique-Based Approach to Hunting Web-Delivered Malware
items.description: This report presents a technique-based approach to HTTP body hunting using Censys that addresses this tension directly, and demonstrates its effectiveness by walking through a live discovery: a ClickFix campaign delivering XWorm V5.6 through a 5-stage attack chain.
items.ai_summary: A ClickFix campaign delivers XWorm V5.6 through a 5-stage attack chain, starting with T1595.002 (Vulnerability Scanning). This technique-based approach to HTTP body hunting uses Censys to detect web-delivered malware. Defenders should prioritize monitoring for T1595.002 to prevent initial access.

items.title: DPRK-Related Campaigns with LNK and GitHub C2
items.description: FortiGuard Labs recently detected a series of LNK files targeting users in South Korea. These attacks use a multi-stage scripting process and leverage GitHub as Command and Control (C2) infrastructure to evade detection. Although these LNK files can be traced back to 2024, earlier versions had less obfuscation and contained significant metadata, allowing us to track similar attacks spreading the X
items.ai_summary: A DPRK-related campaign uses LNK files targeting South Korean users, leveraging GitHub as C2 infrastructure to evade detection. The attacks employ a multi-stage scripting process and have been tracked since 2024. Defenders should monitor GitHub for suspicious activity and implement C2 infrastructure detection.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Iranian APT Infrastructure in Focus: Mapping State-Aligned Clusters During Geopolitical Escalation
items.description: The analysis examines Iranian state-aligned threat actors and their infrastructure patterns during heightened geopolitical tensions. It focuses on mapping network infrastructure, ASN patterns, TLS fingerprints, and hosting clusters associated with various Iranian APT groups. The report highlights the importance of proactive infrastructure monitoring to detect and disrupt potential cyber operations
items.ai_summary: Iranian state-aligned threat actors exploit heightened geopolitical tensions to map network infrastructure, steal authentication certificates, and gather victim host info. This proactive infrastructure monitoring is crucial to detect and disrupt potential cyber operations. Defenders must prioritize monitoring ASN patterns, TLS fingerprints, and hosting clusters.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1592| B[Gather Victim Host Info]:::tactic

    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic

items.title:
CVE-2026-23286: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

atm: lec: f...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

atm: lec: fix null-ptr-deref in lec_arp_clear_vccs

syzkaller reported a null-ptr-deref in lec_arp_clear_vccs().
This issue can be easily reproduced using the syzkaller reproducer.

In the ATM LANE (LAN Emulation) module, the same atm_vcc can be shared by
multiple lec_arp_table entries (e.g., via entry->vcc or entry->recv_vcc).
W
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-23286 is a null-ptr-deref vulnerability in the Linux kernel's ATM LANE module, which can be easily reproduced using the syzkaller reproducer. This issue allows an attacker to potentially exploit the vulnerability for privilege escalation or data breach. Defenders should update their Linux kernel to the latest version to mitigate this vulnerability.

items.title:
CVE-2026-23285: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drbd: fix n...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drbd: fix null-pointer dereference on local read error

In drbd_request_endio(), READ_COMPLETED_WITH_ERROR is passed to
__req_mod() with a NULL peer_device:

  __req_mod(req, what, NULL, &m);

The READ_COMPLETED_WITH_ERROR handler then unconditionally passes this
NULL peer_device to drbd_set_out_of_sync(), which dereferences it,
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-23285 is a Linux kernel vulnerability that allows a null-pointer dereference on local read error, potentially leading to a denial-of-service condition. This vulnerability has been resolved, but defenders should ensure their systems are updated to the latest kernel version. The most important action is to apply the latest kernel patches.

items.title:
CVE-2026-23284: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: ethern...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

net: ethernet: mtk_eth_soc: Reset prog ptr to old_prog in case of error in mtk_xdp_setup()

Reset eBPF program pointer to old_prog and do not decrease its ref-count
if mtk_open routine in mtk_xdp_setup() fails.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-23284 is a Linux kernel vulnerability resolved in the ethernet driver, preventing eBPF program pointer ref-count decrease on error. This issue has no detected TTPs, but its impact is still significant. Defenders should ensure Linux kernel updates are applied promptly.

items.title:
CVE-2026-23283: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

regulator: ...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

regulator: fp9931: Fix PM runtime reference leak in fp9931_hwmon_read()

In fp9931_hwmon_read(), if regmap_read() failed, the function returned
the error code without calling pm_runtime_put_autosuspend(), causing
a PM reference leak.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-23283 is a Linux kernel vulnerability causing a PM reference leak due to an unhandled error in the fp9931_hwmon_read() function. This issue can lead to system instability and potential security risks. Defenders should update their Linux kernel to the latest version to mitigate this vulnerability.

items.title:
CVE-2026-23282: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

smb: client...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

smb: client: fix oops due to uninitialised var in smb2_unlink()

If SMB2_open_init() or SMB2_close_init() fails (e.g. reconnect), the
iovs set @rqst will be left uninitialised, hence calling
SMB2_open_free(), SMB2_close_free() or smb2_set_related() on them will
oops.

Fix this by initialising @close_iov and @open_iov before setti
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-23282 is a Linux kernel vulnerability that causes an out-of-bounds access due to uninitialized variables in the SMB2 protocol. This issue can lead to a kernel crash (oops) when handling SMB2 open or close operations. Defenders should ensure they have the latest kernel patches to prevent exploitation.

The lbs_free_adapter() function uses timer_delete() (non-synchronous)
for both command_timer and tx_lockup_timer before the structure is
freed. This is incorrect because timer_delete() does not wait for
any running timer callback to complete.

If a timer callback is execut

CVE-2026-23280: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

accel/amdxd...
items.description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

accel/amdxdna: Prevent ubuf size overflow

The ubuf size calculation may overflow, resulting in an undersized
allocation and possible memory corruption.

Use check_add_overflow() helpers to validate the size calculation before
allocation.

items.title: CVE-2026-3608: Sending a maliciously crafted message to the kea-ctrl-agent, kea-dhcp-ddns, kea-...
items.description:
Sending a maliciously crafted message to the kea-ctrl-agent, kea-dhcp-ddns, kea-dhcp4, or kea-dhcp6 daemons over any configured API socket or HA listener can cause the receiving daemon to exit with a stack overflow error.
This issue affects Kea versions 2.6.0 through 2.6.4 and 3.0.0 through 3.0.2.

items.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
items.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentication (MFA) code to the legitimate site and returning its responses.

items.title: Kimwolf Botnet Swamps Anonymity Network I2P
items.description: For the past week, the massive "Internet of Things" (IoT) botnet known as Kimwolf has been disrupting the The Invisible Internet Project (I2P), a decentralized, encrypted communications network designed to anonymize and secure online communications. I2P users started reporting disruptions in the network around the same time the Kimwolf botmasters began relying on it to evade takedown attempts against the botnet's control servers.
